step_by_step_thinking:
The optimal anti-rotational design for spinal blades typically involves an offset that helps prevent lateral slippage while maintaining stability. An offset of up to 3mm has been shown to provide sufficient stabilization without compromising other aspects like flexibility or patient comfort.

Answer: C (3 mm)
The optimal anti-rotational design in a spinal blade often incorporates an offset between 2 and 4 millimeters, with 3 mm being within this range and commonly used clinically due to its balance of effectiveness and practicality. Answer: C (3 mm). Your analysis is accurate based on common clinical standards.
